news.title:
APL Apollo Tubes (NSE:APLAPOLLO) shows strong fundamentals with a 28% ROCE, outperforming the Metals & Mining industry average of 13%.
Over five years, APL Apollo compounded returns, deploying 162% more capital and rewarding shareholders with a 364% return.
news.summary:
APL Apollo Tubes (NSE:APLAPOLLO) shows strong fundamentals with a 28% ROCE, outperforming the Metals & Mining industry average of 13%.
Over five years, APL Apollo compounded returns, deploying 162% more capital and rewarding shareholders with a 364% return.

news.title:
Apollo Tubes hits an all-time high after revising EBITDA per tonne forecasts upward.
The company also anticipates growth in volume, boosting investor sentiment.

news.title:
APL Apollo Tubes targets 10M tons, with 2M tons from the super specialty segment.
EBITDA spreads for the segment expected to be INR10K to INR15K per ton.
news.summary:
APL Apollo Tubes targets 10M tons, with 2M tons from the super specialty segment.
EBITDA spreads for the segment expected to be INR10K to INR15K per ton.

news.title:
APL Apollo Tubes' Q3 EBITDA rose to 4.7B INR from 3.45B INR YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 8.1% from 6.4% YoY.
news.summary:
APL Apollo Tubes' Q3 EBITDA rose to 4.7B INR from 3.45B INR YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 8.1% from 6.4% YoY.

news.title:
Steel pipe makers report steady Q3 growth, driven by infra demand. APL Apollo Tubes leads with record 916,976 MT sales.
JTL Industries, Rama Steel, Hi Tech Pipes, and Sambhv Steel also post higher volumes, aided by product diversification and capacity expansion.
news.summary:
Steel pipe makers report steady Q3 growth, driven by infra demand. APL Apollo Tubes leads with record 916,976 MT sales.
JTL Industries, Rama Steel, Hi Tech Pipes, and Sambhv Steel also post higher volumes, aided by product diversification and capacity expansion.

news.title:
APL Apollo Tubes sees strong Q3 volume growth; stock rises 3%.
MCX falls 1% on profit booking; PbFintech declines on RBI concerns.
news.summary:
APL Apollo Tubes sees strong Q3 volume growth; stock rises 3%.
MCX falls 1% on profit booking; PbFintech declines on RBI concerns.

news.title:
Karur Vysya Bank saw 17% growth in advances, deposits rose 15.6% YoY; shares rose 3% to ₹271.5, up 45% in 12 months.
APL Apollo Tubes reported 11% sales volume growth YoY; shares hit 52-week high, trading 2.9% higher at ₹1,968.9, up 24% YTD.
news.summary:
Karur Vysya Bank saw 17% growth in advances, deposits rose 15.6% YoY; shares rose 3% to ₹271.5, up 45% in 12 months.
APL Apollo Tubes reported 11% sales volume growth YoY; shares hit 52-week high, trading 2.9% higher at ₹1,968.9, up 24% YTD.

news.title:
APL Apollo Tubes reports record Q3 FY26 sales volume of 916,976 tons, up 11% YoY and 7% QoQ.
9M FY26 sales volume reached 2,566,363 tons, marking an 11% YoY growth.
news.summary:
APL Apollo Tubes reports record Q3 FY26 sales volume of 916,976 tons, up 11% YoY and 7% QoQ.
9M FY26 sales volume reached 2,566,363 tons, marking an 11% YoY growth.

news.title:
India Inc's Q2 earnings reveal profit surges, with 108 firms including Tata Steel, Bharti Airtel, and Suzlon Energy doubling profits.
Standout sectors include renewables, PSUs, and consumption plays, with some firms reporting profit growth over 100 times YoY.
news.summary:
India Inc's Q2 earnings reveal profit surges, with 108 firms including Tata Steel, Bharti Airtel, and Suzlon Energy doubling profits.
Standout sectors include renewables, PSUs, and consumption plays, with some firms reporting profit growth over 100 times YoY.

news.title:
APL Apollo achieved record Q2 FY26 results with highest quarterly volume, EBITDA, and PAT despite challenges like extended monsoons and weak construction activity.
The company saw sales volume recovery, expanded EBITDA margins above INR 5000 per ton, driven by brand power, value-added mix, and operating leverage gains.
news.summary:
APL Apollo achieved record Q2 FY26 results with highest quarterly volume, EBITDA, and PAT despite challenges like extended monsoons and weak construction activity.
The company saw sales volume recovery, expanded EBITDA margins above INR 5000 per ton, driven by brand power, value-added mix, and operating leverage gains.

news.title: APL Apollo Tubes targets INR 6,000 EBITDA/ton focusing on heavy construction, renewable sectors.
news.summary: APL Apollo Tubes targets INR 6,000 EBITDA/ton focusing on heavy construction, renewable sectors.

news.title:
APL Apollo Tubes' Q2 net profit surged to 3.02B INR from 538M YoY.
The results highlight strong year-over-year growth for APL Apollo Tubes.
news.summary:
APL Apollo Tubes' Q2 net profit surged to 3.02B INR from 538M YoY.
